basics.name: Juno Kim
basics.label: Computer Science Student
basics.summary: Computer Science student at the University of Pennsylvania specializing in signal processing, optimization, and AI/ML. Full-stack developer with experience building Chrome extensions, web applications, and machine learning systems. Track record of securing grants and patents for innovative projects.

projects.name: Taurus
projects.description: Chrome extension injecting Polymarket prediction markets into X/Twitter
highlights: Built Chrome extension (Manifest V3) using React + Vite with Shadow DOM integration
Developed Fastify/TypeScript backend with 40% keyword + 60% semantic scoring
Implemented AI-powered auto-exit rules and real-time PnL monitoring

projects.name: Path @ Penn Redesign
projects.description: UX redesign of Penn's course registration system
highlights: Conducted user interviews identifying key UX problems
Designed solution focusing on workflow optimization
Built interactive Figma prototype with improved navigation

projects.name: VeriRAG
projects.description: Open standard and Python library for tamper-proof AI audit trails
highlights: Created cryptographic verification system using Merkle trees
Designed for EU AI Act Article 12 compliance
Built 3-line integration with CLI verifier and LangChain callbacks

projects.name: DealScout
projects.description: Chrome Extension for real estate listing analysis
highlights: Developed Chrome extension for ROI analysis of Zillow listings
Secured $27K+ in grant funding at 2025 Bittensor Endgame Summit
Integrated Gemini LLM for property insights chat interface

projects.name: Pennstagram
projects.description: Instagram clone + RAG chatbot — NETS 2120 final project
highlights: Built scalable Instagram-style web app with React and Kafka
Implemented personalized content ranking using Spark-based algorithms
Integrated RAG chatbot with LangChain and ChromaDB

projects.name: Lucidity
projects.description: Chrome Extension using ML to block unproductive websites
highlights: Boosted student productivity by 24% through ML-powered website blocking
Built TF-IDF classification model with NumPy, Pandas, and NLTK
Achieved provisional patent (No. 63005219) and multiple awards
